title: Coffee with Cream on Wool
timeEstimate: Pro-lane within hours; one home round max
stainChemistry: Coffee with cream is three-part on wool: tannin + protein + fat (butterfat). Wool is wool protein, banned tools include enzymes, hot water, alkaline (felts wool + locks tannin), agitation (felts on contact when wet). The home toolkit shrinks to: cold water + Dawn or wool-safe detergent + dilute vinegar. Repeat-round chemistry on wool is the #1 felting failure mode.
whyThisWorks: Wool specialist with cold NSD + timed acetic acid + dilute peroxide can lift fresh combo without felting. Home protocol: ONE round of cold + wool-safe detergent + 60-sec vinegar, then stop. Repeat home rounds felt the fiber more often than they lift residual coffee.
defaultAssumption: Wool + coffee-with-cream = pro within hours. ONE careful home round, then stop. Cappuccino / latte (more cream) = even earlier escalation.

spottingProtocol.instruction: Press cold water on the BACK with vertical pressure. No rubbing. Replace cloth as it picks up brown.
Cold NSD sponge press. Suspends remaining tannin + milk protein + fat without alkalinity that would weaken keratin.
Rinse until water runs clear. Residual NSD interferes with later steps.
Diluted acetic acid via dropper for residual color. STRICT 60-sec dwell. Flush cold immediately.

spottingProtocol.agent: Dilute Hydrogen Peroxide for Oxidized Residue
spottingProtocol.instruction: For oxidized brown, dilute peroxide 1–2 min monitored. Flush cold immediately.
Rinse until clear. Press between dry towels — never wring. Lay flat to air-dry, reshape damp.

steps: 5. Dilute H₂O₂ for oxidized residue, 1–2 min monitored.
6. Final cold rinse, press flat to air-dry.
professionalProtocol.temperature: cold throughout — wool felts above 30°C when wet
warnings: NEVER hot water — felts wool + sets tannin + sets milk protein
NEVER enzymes — digests wool keratin AND milk protein equally
60-sec ceiling on acid; 1–2 min on peroxide

homeSolutions.agent: ONE Round Max — Felt Risk Is the Real Failure
homeSolutions.instruction: Coffee-with-cream on wool stacks three failure modes: heat felts wool AND sets the milk protein AND sets the tannin. Alkaline cleaners (OxiClean, baking soda) felt wool AND lock tannin. Enzymes digest wool. Each wrong move stacks on the others. The one-round home rule on wool is non-negotiable. If your one careful cold-water round doesn't clear it, pro is the answer.

homeSolutions.agent: Cold Water Press-Blot from the BACK
homeSolutions.instruction: Turn inside out. Place white towel under the stain. Press a SECOND white cloth dampened in COLD/ice water onto the BACK. Vertical pressure only — no rubbing. Lift, refresh cloth, press again. Cold ONLY — heat felts wool AND sets the milk protein AND sets the tannin in one bad move.

Mix half a teaspoon of Eucalan, Soak, or Woolite (or plain clear Dawn if that's what you have) in two cups COLD water. Dip a white cloth, wring nearly dry, PRESS on the stain. 3 minutes dwell. Blot. ONE PASS. Do NOT use OxiClean, Biz, or Zout — they contain enzymes that digest wool.

Mix 1 part white vinegar with 1 part cool water. Press a cotton swab on the stain for 60 SECONDS MAX — set a timer. Flush with cool water immediately. Vinegar handles residual tannin and restores wool's natural pH (~5).

Press wool between two dry white towels — stack, press, lift. No wringing or twisting. Lay flat on fresh towel, reshape damp, air-dry at room temperature out of sun. NEVER hang wet wool. NEVER apply heat.

homeSolutions.agent: Pro Within Hours If Anything Remains
homeSolutions.instruction: Take to a wool specialist (call ahead — not every cleaner handles wool). Tell them: "Coffee with cream on wool, age [hours], ONE round at home with cold water + [Eucalan / Dawn] + 60-sec vinegar. No hot water, no enzymes, no OxiClean, no peroxide. [Residual brown]."

steps: 1. Wool + coffee-cream = ONE home round max.
2. Cold water press-blot from the BACK (no rubbing).
3. One pass with Eucalan / Soak / Dawn, 3 min cold.
5. Press dry between towels, lay flat air-dry.
6. Anything remaining = pro. NO second home round.
products: Eucalan / Soak / Woolite OR clear Dawn
diyProtocol.whenToStop: Stop after one careful round. Repeat home chemistry on wool felts the fiber.
warnings: NEVER hot water — felts wool + sets milk protein + sets tannin

neverDo: Never agitate or scrub wet wool
fiberSensitivities: Wool keratin felts when wet + warm + agitated
fiber bonds re-lock together irreversibly under heat
Enzymes digest keratin equally with milk protein

materialWarnings: ONE round home max — repeat chemistry felts wool
Three failure modes stack: heat sets all three components

escalation.when: After one careful home round, regardless of result. Earlier for cappuccino / latte (more cream), large spills, or high-value garments.
escalation.whatToTell: "Coffee with cream on wool, age [hours], one round at home with cold water + [wool-safe detergent] + 60-sec vinegar. No hot water, no enzymes, no peroxide. [Residual brown / fully clear]."
escalation.specialistType: professional dry cleaner specializing in wool (call ahead — confirm acetic acid + dilute peroxide spotting capability)

contextQuestions: Cappuccino / latte (more cream) or splash-of-cream coffee?
How long ago — fresh, hours, or older?
Wool type — merino / regular knit / blend?
Has anyone used hot water / OxiClean / agitation already?
deepSolveHooks.modifierNotes: More cream = more protein + fat = harder. Merino is finer + felts faster. Pre-applied OxiClean = wool + tannin both compromised; disclose to pro.
